content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 27, 199 o
Roberta L. weintraub
Board of Educati.on
city of Los Angeles
P.O. Box 33o7
Los Angeles, CA 9 0051
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-210
Dear Ms. weintraubs
This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibil ities under the new tlpersonal use'r provisions
of -tire Political Reform Act (the rrasXtrl .1/
OUESTION
uay canpaign funds be used to pay for your travel to and
tuition for an educational prograD for senior state and IocaI
government executives focusing on iuproving adninistrative skills?
CONCLUSION
Expenditures made for a Program for public officials 
intended
to inprove their adninistrative skilIs and to provide training to
be used in the perfornance of the official's 
governmental duties
are directly related to a political or governmental purpose.
consequently, campaign funds may be used to pay to attend the
prograB.
FACTS
You have asked whether you Itray use canpaign funds to pay for
travel and tuition (which includes acconmodations) to a sunmer
program offered by Harvard University entitled rrThe Program for
senior Executives in State and Local GovernlIentir (the rrprogrramrr) .
The progEaD is for senior executives in state and Ioca1 government
L/ Government code sections
ences are to the Government
81000-91015. A11 statutory refer-
code unless otherwise i,ndicated.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


File No. A-9o-21o
Page 2
and thelr elected counterparts and runs three weeks. The progran
is designed to help participants sharpen their problem solving
skills, gain insight into sociaL and econornic forces that shape
their operational environment, examine their ethical and profes-
sional responsibilities, understand the role of public executives
in irnproving productivity and nanaging human resources. Tuitj.on
for the program is $6,8o0.
ANALYSIS
Effective January L, L99o, the Act was amended by senate BilI
1431 to include new provisions that regulate the appropriate use
of carnpaign funds. (section 85800, et seq., the I'Persona1 userl
Ia!r. ) The purpose of the legislation was rrto ensure that
candidates, elected officers and the people clearly recognize ac-
ceptable and unacceptable uses of canpaign funds'rt (senate Elec-
tions Conmittee Analysis of SB 1431.)
The general rule of the new personal use Law is that any
expenditure of canpaign funds rnust be, at a ninirnum, reasonably
related to a political, 
Iegislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 85801.) However, under some circumstances the personal
use 1aw requires a stricter standard. For example, where an
expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on the
candidate or officehotder, the expenditure must be directly
r;I;a;a to a political, 
Iegislative, or governrnental purpole.2/
(section 85801. )
Tuit ion
clearly, where campaign funds are used to pay a fee for your
attendance at an educational program, you have received a personal
benefit equivalent to the pa)rment. According to the facts you
provided, the tuition for the prograln is greater than $100.
consequently, for the expenditure of carnpaign funds to be permis-
sible there must be a direct relationship between the expenditure
and a politicat, 
legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section
85801. )
Since the progran at Harvard is clearly offered for public
officials, 
and the training provided to the participants will be
used in the performance of their governmental duties, we believe
there is a direct relationship between the expenditure of canpaign
funds for the progran and a political or goverru[enta1 purpose.
consequently, caEpaign funds may be used to pay tuition for the
Progrram.
2/ A substantial personal
benefit to the candidate or
tion 85800 (b) (2 ) . )
benefit is defined as a direct personal
officeholder of $100 or nore. (sec-


File No. A-90-210
Page 3
Travel To and From the Proqram
Section 85802 (a) provides:
Campaign funds shaIl not be used to pay or
reirnburse the candidate, the elected officer, or
employees or staff of the canpaign commi.ttee or the
elected officer's governnental agency for travel
expcrnses and necessary accommodations except when these
expenditures are directly related to a political,
leqislative. or oovernmental purpose.
(Emphasis added. )
Therefore, the use of carnpaign funds to pay for transporta-
tion to and from the site of the progran is permissible, provided
the progran is directly related to a political, 
legislative, or
governmental purpose. Since we concluded above that the program
is directly related to a politlcal or governmental purpose associ-
ated rrith your duties as a board nenber for the Los Angeles Uni-
fied School District, 
payment for travel to and from the progran
is also pernissible.
If you have any further questions r-egarding this matter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
KED: JWW: p1h
John w. wallace
counsel, Legal Division
